title: Add domain separation tags to all nullifier hash computations

artifacts.description: Prepend DOMAIN_HUMAN constant (e.g. 0x48554d414e = ASCII 'HUMAN' as field element) as first input to Poseidon2 nullifier hash: Poseidon3(DOMAIN_HUMAN, scope, secret)
Prepend DOMAIN_AGENT constant as first input to Poseidon2 nullifier hash: Poseidon3(DOMAIN_AGENT, secret, sessionNonce)
Prepend DOMAIN_DELEG constant as first input to any nullifier hash in the delegation circuit: Poseidon3(DOMAIN_DELEG, ...inputs)
Shared include file defining DOMAIN_HUMAN, DOMAIN_AGENT, DOMAIN_DELEG as BN254 field-safe constants. Single source of truth to avoid divergence.

Test that nullifiers for human and agent with identical raw inputs (scope==secret, secret==sessionNonce) differ after domain tag introduction. Also verify no regression on valid witness generation for all three circuits.
Update nullifier computation section to document domain tag constants and their rationale. Add formal note that nullifier spaces are disjoint by construction.
Add formal property: NullifierDomainDisjointness — for any inputs, H_human(...) != H_agent(...) != H_deleg(...). Reference domain tag constants.
If the TS SDK computes or validates nullifier hashes outside the circuit (e.g. for indexing), update to include domain tag in the same Poseidon3 arrangement.

steps: Audit all three circuits (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation) to enumerate every Poseidon call that produces a nullifier hash and record current input arity
Choose domain tag constants: pick three distinct BN254 field elements well outside the range of normal inputs (e.g. keccak256('bolyra.human.nullifier.v1') mod p, same pattern for agent and deleg)
Create circuits/src/DomainTags.circom exporting DOMAIN_HUMAN, DOMAIN_AGENT, DOMAIN_DELEG as template-less signal constants (or plain circom `var` values)
Update HumanUniqueness.circom: include DomainTags.circom, change Poseidon2(scope, secret) to Poseidon3(DOMAIN_HUMAN, scope, secret), verify output signal name unchanged
Update AgentPolicy.circom: change Poseidon2(secret, sessionNonce) to Poseidon3(DOMAIN_AGENT, secret, sessionNonce)
Update Delegation.circom: identify nullifier computation(s) and prepend DOMAIN_DELEG constant in the same manner
Recompile all three circuits via `npm run compile:circuits` and confirm R1CS constraint counts increase by ~3 per nullifier hash (one additional Poseidon round)
Update circuit test witnesses in circuits/test/ to pass the new expected nullifier values (existing witnesses will now be stale)
Write nullifier_domain_separation.test.js: generate witnesses where raw inputs overlap across circuit types and assert nullifiers are distinct
Run full test suite: `npm run test:circuits:fast` for witness-only, then `npm run test:circuits:slow` for full proof regression
Audit sdk/src/nullifier.ts (and any Python sdk-python equivalent) for off-circuit nullifier computation; apply matching Poseidon3 with domain tag
Update spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md with the new nullifier computation formula and domain tag constant values
Update circuits/FORMAL-PROPERTIES.md with the NullifierDomainDisjointness property
Regenerate trusted setup artifacts if necessary (pot16.ptau is reused but per-circuit .zkey files must be regenerated after circuit changes)
Regenerate Solidity verifier contracts from new .vkey.json files via snarkjs and verify Hardhat tests still pass
DCO-sign commit: `git commit -s -m 'feat(circuits): add domain separation tags to all nullifier hash computations'`
dependencies: circomlib Poseidon3 template (or equivalent arity-3 variant available in circomlib)
pot16.ptau — must accommodate any constraint increase (2^16 budget; +6 constraints is negligible)
Existing Poseidon2 usage confirmed in HumanUniqueness and AgentPolicy before patching Delegation
